mission.title: caddy: Unable to issue IP address certificate with Let's Encrypt `shortlived` ACME profile
mission.description: Unable to issue IP address certificate with Let's Encrypt `shortlived` ACME profile. This issue affects 8+ users.

steps.title: Identify caddy troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your caddy installation:
```bash
caddy version
caddy validate --config /etc/caddy/Caddyfile
```
Look for errors or warnings that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review caddy configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant caddy configuration:
### Issue Details

Greetings,

[Let's encrypt has rolled out IP address Identifiers for shortlived certificates to General

steps.title: Apply the fix for Unable to issue IP address certificate with Let's Encrypt…
steps.description:
As of 5 months ago the code was updated to allow LE for issuing IP certs:

```go
	publicCAsAndIPCerts := map[string]bool{ // map of public CAs to whether they support IP certificates (last updated: Q1 2024)
		"api.letsencrypt.org": true,  //
```yaml
Then the IP address configuration:
```

steps.title: Upgrade caddy to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade caddy:
```bash
# Check current version
caddy version
# Follow the project's upgrade guide at https://github.com/caddyserver/caddy
```

steps.title: Confirm Unable to issue IP address certificate with Let's… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test caddy to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
As of 5 months ago the code was updated to allow LE for issuing IP certs:

```go
	publicCAsAndIPCerts := map[string]bool{ // map of public CAs to whether they support IP certificates (last updated: Q1 2024)
		"api.letsencrypt.org": true,  // https://letsencrypt.org/2025/07/01/issuing-our-first-ip-address-certificate/
		"acme.zerossl.com":    false, // only supported via their API, not ACME
codeSnippets:
Seems upgrading caddy manually with `caddy upgrade` fixed the issue for Ipv4 IP Identifiers. 

However, for IPv6, I get the following:

Its seems like 'tls-alpn-01' challenge types successful authorized, but 'http-01' not.
Caddyfile:

prerequisites.description: A working caddy installation or development environment.
